# Style Guide: src/content/docs/fwvt

Use this guide when proofreading or drafting pages in `src/content/docs/fwvt`.

## Audience and Purpose
- Audience: beginners to early-intermediate developers learning by example.
- Purpose: teach View Transition concepts through an episodic, motivational format.

## Tone Snapshot
- Welcoming, enthusiastic, and narrative.
- Motivational without losing technical correctness.
- More expressive than all other docs directories.

## Voice Rules
- Keep an instructor-host voice (guided and upbeat).
- Invite experimentation and curiosity.
- Explain concepts in simple terms before deeper technical detail.

## Structure Conventions
- Episode-style flow with clear progression.
- Learning-outcome bullets are common.
- Media and demo references are central; text often frames what to look for.
- Calls to action and transitions between sections should feel energetic.

## Language and Terminology
- Technical terms are introduced gently, often with plain-language paraphrases.
- Keep all identifiers and API names exact in code and inline snippets.
- Metaphoric phrasing is acceptable when it supports comprehension.

## Sentence and Punctuation Patterns
- More conversational and expressive sentence rhythm.
- Rhetorical questions and occasional exclamation marks are normal.
- American spelling is common in existing content.

## Proofreading Priorities
- Preserve the inviting teaching voice while fixing grammar and clarity.
- Do not flatten the text into dry reference prose.
- Keep list structure, media captions, and episode sequencing intact.
- Tighten duplicated phrases and obvious wording slips.

## Fit Check
Draft matches `fwvt` style if it reads like a guided episode: clear, warm, and energetic, with technically accurate instructions.
